sources.notes: Shared Div.2 entry; canonical problem is contest 2138D.
Official topic confirms the shifted-position representation and threshold/counting approach.
Independent writeup used to cross-check the sorting and threshold decomposition.

annotation.summary: Count or compute outcomes of slider movements over a permutation-like state.
annotation.constraints: The number of sliders/operations is large, so simulating every final state directly is infeasible.
annotation.core_idea: Shift each slider position by its index to normalize movement constraints. The resulting states are determined by thresholded maximum/minimum/assignment effects. Sort the normalized values and decompose the counting by thresholds so each range contributes in bulk.
tricks: Normalize positions by subtracting the index.
Represent operations as thresholded effects on normalized values.
Sort and count ranges instead of simulating each configuration.

solution_variants.name: shifted-threshold-counting
solution_variants.summary: Transform positions to b_i = position_i - i, sort them, and count the final effects by threshold ranges.

tags.evidence: The solution counts possible/order-dependent outcomes over normalized permutation-like positions.
The original slider positions are compressed into normalized b_i values that preserve all needed information.
The final count is decomposed by threshold ranges of the normalized values.
tags.description: Split a counting or decision problem by threshold ranges so all values in one range have the same contribution rule.
tags.created_reason: Threshold-range decomposition is a reusable counting trick that is more precise than generic sorting or contribution maintenance.

tags.evidence: Sorting normalized values enables range counting by thresholds.
The normalized threshold ranges are combined by counting formulas.
